sections.heading: Prepositions of Place — Complete Guide
sections.body:
AT — specific point (small, precise):
at the door, at the bus stop, at the corner, at school, at work, at home

ON — surface or line:
on the table, on the wall, on the floor, on the bus/train/plane, on the street, on a team

IN — enclosed space or area:
in the room, in the box, in Delhi, in India, in the world, in the water, in the morning

Special cases:
• 'at home' (not 'in home') — fixed expression
• 'on the bus' (not 'in the bus') — British English uses 'on' for public transport
• 'in the car' (not 'on the car') — private vehicles use 'in'
• 'at night' (not 'in night') — fixed expression
• 'on the weekend' (American) vs 'at the weekend' (British)

sections.heading: Prepositions of Time — Complete Guide
sections.body:
AT — specific moments:
at 3 o'clock, at noon, at midnight, at sunrise, at lunchtime, at the moment

ON — days and dates:
on Monday, on July 17th, on my birthday, on Christmas Day, on the first of May

IN — periods and longer durations:
in the morning/afternoon/evening, in January, in 2026, in summer, in the 21st century, in two weeks

FOR vs SINCE:
for + duration: for 3 hours, for a long time, for two years
since + point: since Monday, since 9am, since last year, since I was a child

BY — deadline:
by Friday (before Friday), by next week, by 5pm

DURING vs FOR:
during + event: during the meeting, during summer vacation
for + duration: for three hours, for ten years

UNTIL/TILL — up to a point:
I'll wait until 5pm. / I'll wait till tomorrow.

sections.heading: Conjunctions Deep Dive
sections.body:
Coordinating conjunctions (FANBOYS):
For, And, Nor, But, Or, Yet, So
Connect equal elements:
'I like tea, but I prefer coffee.'
'She studied hard, so she passed.'

Subordinating conjunctions:
Cause: because, since, as
Contrast: although, though, even though, while, whereas
Time: when, while, before, after, until, as soon as
Condition: if, unless, provided that
Purpose: so that, in order that

Common mistakes:
❌ 'Because I was tired, so I slept.' (don't use both!)
✅ 'Because I was tired, I slept.' OR 'I was tired, so I slept.'

❌ 'Although it was cold, but we went out.'
✅ 'Although it was cold, we went out.'

sections.heading: Preposition Practice
sections.body:
Fill in the correct preposition:
1. I'll meet you ___ Monday.
2. The book is ___ the table.
3. She lives ___ Mumbai.
4. We've been friends ___ 2019.
5. He arrived ___ 3 o'clock.
6. The picture is ___ the wall.
7. I'll finish ___ Friday.
8. She was born ___ March.
9. They sat ___ each other.
10. The keys are ___ the drawer.

Answers: 1.on 2.on 3.in 4.since 5.at 6.on 7.by 8.in 9.next to 10.in

summary: Prepositions take practice — there's no single rule that covers everything. Learn the main patterns (at=point, on=surface, in=enclosed) and then memorize the exceptions through usage.
